chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | - Must place at least one order to remain active | - Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business

synonyms: one or more Order Line Items
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | - A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | - An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | - An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | | ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO | | | ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO | | | ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Parent order | NO | | - ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | | ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Order being paid | NO | | | ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Order being fulfilled | NO | | **Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | - A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | **Description**: Monetary transactions settling order amounts. | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Payment          → PAYMENT |   ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | | PAYMENT_I

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Related Concepts**: Category, Inventory, OrderLineItem | - "Wireless Bluetooth Headphones - Black" (SKU: ELE-001234) | - "Cotton T-Shirt - Medium, Blue" (SKU: CLO-002345) | - An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | **Business Concept Mapping**: Product | Product          → TB_PRODUCT | - A Product belongs to exactly one Category | - A Product appears on zero or more Order Line Items | **Business Context**: Products are the core items that customers purchase. | Products are the core items that customers purchase. They are organized into categories and have inventory tracked across warehouses. | - Must belong to a valid category before being sold | **Definition**: A tangible or digital item offered for sale on the platform. | Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments.

entities_post_resolution.name: exactly one Category
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - A Product belongs to exactly one Category | - Belongs to exactly one Category

**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order. | **Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | - ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Description**: Physical delivery of products to customers. | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. | They track what items were sent, when, and to whom. | - Unique Shipment ID | - References exactly one Sales Order | - Source Warehouse | - Destination Customer Address | - Shipped timestamp | - Tracking number | - Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | - Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment | - A Sales Order has one or more Shipments | Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments.
